fraud-detection-framework.name: Fraud Detection Framework
fraud-detection-framework.description: Decision Intelligence and Explainable AI (XAI) pipeline for identifying fraudulent transactions in financial datasets.
fraud-detection-framework.problem: Financial institutions lose billions to fraud annually. Traditional rule-based systems miss novel attack patterns and generate excessive false positives.
fraud-detection-framework.whyIBuiltIt: I was fascinated by adversarial dynamics in systems. Fraud is a constantly evolving game of cat and mouse, and I wanted to understand how ML could detect anomalies that humans can't see.
fraud-detection-framework.solution: Architected an XGBoost-based framework with cost-optimization strategies. Developed a high-performance FastAPI inference engine with sub-millisecond latency, SHAP for risk-based explainability, and a 3-tier fallback system (Llama-3 → Mixtral → Rule-based) for transaction justifications.
fraud-detection-framework.architecture: Data Ingestion -> XGBoost -> FastAPI -> SHAP -> LLM Fallback Justification
fraud-detection-framework.challenges: Handling extreme class imbalance in fraud datasets. Only 0.17% of transactions were fraudulent, requiring careful sampling strategies like SMOTE.
fraud-detection-framework.lessonsLearned: Cost-sensitive learning and explainability matter more than raw accuracy. A model that can justify its decisions builds trust.
fraud-detection-framework.results: Achieved a final PR-AUC of 0.833 and 90.9% precision. Reduced test costs by 11.06% ($295.75) and false positives by 38.9% (from 18 instances to 11).

weapon-detection-system.name: Weapon Detection System
weapon-detection-system.description: Real-time computer vision system for detecting weapons in video feeds using deep learning object detection.
weapon-detection-system.problem: Security monitoring relies on human operators watching multiple feeds simultaneously, leading to missed threats and slow response times.
weapon-detection-system.whyIBuiltIt: I wanted to push my understanding of computer vision beyond static images and into real-time inference constraints, applying AI to physical security.
weapon-detection-system.solution: Built a real-time detection pipeline using a custom YOLOv8 model. Deployed a robust inference pipeline with dynamic confidence thresholds to ensure reliable detection across varied environmental conditions.
weapon-detection-system.architecture: Camera Feed -> Frame Processing -> YOLOv8 Model -> Dynamic Thresholding -> Alert System
weapon-detection-system.challenges: Achieving reliable detection under varying lighting conditions and camera angles. Reducing false positives from objects that visually resemble weapons.
weapon-detection-system.lessonsLearned: Data quality and augmentation strategy matters far more than model architecture for real-world computer vision deployments.
weapon-detection-system.results: Achieved 91.5% mAP@0.5, 87.3% precision, and an 88.0% recall rate while minimizing false positives, optimizing training on an RTX 4060 GPU.

disaster-relief-app.name: Disaster Relief App
disaster-relief-app.description: Mobile-first application connecting disaster-affected communities with relief resources and volunteer coordination.
disaster-relief-app.problem: During disasters, affected communities struggle to find resources while relief organizations lack real-time ground truth data for effective deployment.
disaster-relief-app.whyIBuiltIt: Participating in a hackathon, I saw a gap in how quickly chaotic information is routed during crises. I wanted to build a resilient, offline-capable tool.
disaster-relief-app.solution: Created a geolocation-based platform that matches resource requests with nearby volunteers and NGOs, with real-time status tracking and priority routing.
disaster-relief-app.architecture: Mobile App -> API Gateway -> Node.js Backend -> MongoDB -> Maps API
disaster-relief-app.challenges: Designing for unreliable network conditions in disaster zones. Building offline-first data sync that works on low-bandwidth connections.
disaster-relief-app.lessonsLearned: Build for the worst-case scenario first. Offline capability and low-bandwidth optimization should be core architecture decisions, not afterthoughts.
disaster-relief-app.results: Awarded 1st place at the local hackathon for technical execution and practical utility.
